reasoning: NOAA's statistical analysis from their July 2025 Global Climate Report explicitly stated there is "less than a 1% chance" of 2025 being the warmest year on record. While January 2025 set a record, subsequent months (like April, May, and June) consistently trailed 2024 as La Niña conditions took hold. Carbon Brief similarly reported in mid-2025 that the year was on track to be only the second or third warmest. Given the mathematical tracking through mid-to-late 2025, it is virtually impossible for 2025 to surpass 2024. I am predicting 0.02, which matches the market price and the lowest bound of confidence allowed by the schema.
